behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 considered Integration (watcher module + pipeline wiring). Pass 2 upgrades to System because the failure boundary is real filesystem event delivery for nested paths (recursive watch behavior varies by OS/watcher backend), which mocks can hide. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE are both yes. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely filesystem watching; no AI/ML output dependency, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temporary directory tree. Create `watched/sub/a.txt` after startup and assert exactly one completed pipeline output appears in the output directory for that file within timeout.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 considered Integration because dedup often sits between watcher and queue. Pass 2 lowers to Unit: behavior text targets a single rule (same path + time window) and does not require real filesystem/network/subprocess. A fake clock and in-memory state can reproduce the failure precisely. Rejected higher levels with concrete text: 'deduplication logic' and 'same file path within a configurable time window' describe local decision logic, not OS/event semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup component directly with identical normalized path twice under a controlled fake clock, asserting first call accepted and second call rejected until the window expires, then accepted after expiry.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 considered Integration with synthetic duplicate events. Pass 2 keeps System because the behavior explicitly references OS-emitted duplicate events; exact burst shape/coalescing is infrastructure-dependent and mocks can miss real duplicates or timing. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE are yes. Rejected higher levels: no model output or multi-agent steps are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real watched directory, perform a write pattern that triggers duplicate events on the host OS (e.g., write+rename/update), then assert only one processing record/output for that file path.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 already System; pass 2 confirms. This failure appears at OS watcher lifecycle boundaries (inode/handle invalidation on delete/recreate). In-process fakes usually do not replicate real teardown/rebind behavior. INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a) is yes. Rejected higher levels: behavior is filesystem resilience only, with no AI/ML dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon, delete the watched directory, recreate it at the same path, then create a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 considered Integration (queue ordering logic). Pass 2 upgrades to System because correctness depends on real filesystem timestamp metadata and event timing under concurrent arrivals; mocked timestamps/events can hide granularity and race issues seen on real filesystems. REAL-INFRA yes; MOCK-HIDE likely yes. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output quality or agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real watched directory, create several files nearly simultaneously with controlled timestamp ordering, then assert processing completion order matches creation timestamp order (with defined tie-break behavior if equal).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 considered Integration with mocked 'file complete' signals. Pass 2 keeps System: partial-write timing and readiness are real filesystem/OS behaviors (flush, rename, append cadence) that mocks frequently simplify, hiding premature pickup bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a) and MOCK-HIDE are yes. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon, begin a slow/streamed write to a file in the watched directory, verify no processing starts before writer closes, then verify processing starts and succeeds after completion.
